RICHABO H. OINEL

VIA FAX AND ITAI L
Blanca Breeze, Esq.
Staff Attorney
Fair PoIitical Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 600
Sacramento, California 
95812
Dear Ms. Breeze:
U 
A Specific
Government code
r rEfl
Re: Reouest for Fomal written Advice
l-Government Code q 831L4(b) t FPPC
Requlation 18329.l
our office represents the city of La Puente (trCity't) as
City Attorney. I'Iayor Louis R. Perez has authorized us to request
format written advice from the Fair Po1itical Practices
Cornmission (xFPPctt). The question is whether Mayor Perez has a
financial interest which prohibits hin from participating in
decisions involving the proposed Do$/ntown Specific Plan (the
"Specific Plan"). This request is based on the following facts:
The cj-ty has retained consultants who.are working with
staff on developiig a potential Downtown Specific Plan'l/ 
-!,Ie
are infonned that they have a prelirninary working draft, that is
undergoing further reiinement. The boundaries of the Specific
plan ire 6urrently proposed to be the ol-d downto!,/n comrnercial
";;" ;i the city ltir" ;'et.t Area'r) . The general objective is to
upgrade the downtown area into a viable commercial center'
Plan is authorized by, and described in,
Sections 65450-65457.


RICHARDS WATSON & GERSHON
Bl-anca Breeze, Esq.
September 25, 1992
Page 2
The City has scheduled a joint rneeting of the City
Council and Planning Conmission on October L9, 1992. At that
meeting, the initial 
concept for the specific PIan wilt be
presented to the Councj,L, Cornrnission, and public. 
The purpose of
the October 19 meeting j-s for staff and the consultants to obtain
guidance from the Council and Planning Conmission on what
direction the Specific PIan should take. No document wilL be
presented for approval at the October L9 meeting. There will- be
additional meetings involving the Specific Plan before it is
presented to the Cornrnission and ultinately to the Council for
adoption [assuning the proposed Specific PIan ever gets that
farl.
A specific Pl,an does not authorj,ze a public agency to
acquire property for the purpose of implementing the PLan. The
Pl-an is merely a planning docunent which the City hopes wil-l-
gTuide new development. It is anticipated that the Plan will
require that new developnent be constructed in conformity with
the P1an, but that existing buildings can be operated and
repaired even though they are not consistent r,rith the Ptan.
Further, there is no amortization schedule that would require
demolition of any building in the PIan Area. Since the City will
not have the power to condemn property to implement the P1an,
implementation wi]I be dependant upon existing property owners
constructing improvements in compliance with the Plan or upon new
devefopers acguiring property and constructj,ng new buildings
pursuant to the PIanrs objectives. It is likely, therefore, that
it will be rnany years, if ever, before the Pl,an is irnplemented.
We are informed that Mayor Perez I wife is an empl-oyee
of a nomanrs retail clothing business (the ttBusinessrt) located in
the Plan Area. We are further informed that the owner of the
Business also owns the building and real property on v/hich the
Business i,s located, and that the building houses one other
storefront that is currently vacant.
Pursuant to Section 82030(a), his community property
interest in his wife's income is a source of income to Mayor
Perez- we are informed that she earned more than $500 from the
business within the past twelve nonths. eccordingly, his
cornmunity property interest in that income will- exceed $250.
The initial 
concept of the Specific Plan cafls for the
redesign of several blocks in the PIan Area to create central
courtyards and to encourage pedestrian activity. 
one of the
blocks proposed to be redesigned is the block on which the
Business that employs l"lrs, Perez is located. U1tj-nate
implernentation of the redesign of that block would require the
demolition of the building housing the Business. The building
owner j.s not obligated to denolish the building, however. The


RICHARDS WATSON & GERSHON
Blanca Breeze, Esq.
September 25, L992
Page 3
buitding and Business could remain indefinitely if the property
o!'rner does not desire to construct a new building.
Thus, if a property orrrner ever irnplements the redesign
of the block on which the Business is located, implenentation of
the Specific Pfan would likely cause the relocation of the
business that enploys I'Irs. Perez. There is therefore a remote
possibitity that adoption of the Specific Plan vrould lead to the
denolition of the building that houses the Business.
we are troubled by the remoteness of any potential
impact on the Business. Unlike a reCeveloprrent pLan, the
Specific Plan does not grant to the city the authority to buy the
property and demolish the building where it is located. The
Specific Pl,an caI1s for the redesign of various city blocks, but
provides no implementation mechanisn. As l^rith most specific
pIans, the city will have to await efforts from the private
sector in order for the Specific PLan to be inplernented.
Therefore, the impact on the Business which enploys Mrs. Perez is
very renote, speculatj-ve and uncertain. Adoption of the Plan
certainly will have no immediate impact on DIrs. Perezr j.ncome or
the income of the Business. The only possible inpact could be on
the property on which the Business is located, but this impact
would again appear to be too speculative to be quantifiable.
In addition to the Specific Plan, we are al,so informed
that city staff are considering recommending adoption of a
redevelopment project. 
There is currently no redevelopment
project in the City. A redevelopment project, which would have
incLuded the downtown area, was overturned by referendum on 1991.
A redevelopment project nay [or nay not] include the power of
eninent domain. In the event that both the Specific PIan and a
redevelopnent project that includes the power of eminent donain
are adopted, and to the extent that financing is available, the
Specific PIan would be more 1ike1y to be irnplemented.
However, the specific Plan can [and might] be adopted,
if at aIt, without adoption of a redevelopment project. 
There is
no requirement that a specific plan be implenented through a
redevelopnent project, just as there is no reguirement that a
redevelopment project also inctude a specific pl-an.
Accordinqly, !/e reqnlest formal wrj-tten advice from the
Comnission on two questions:
1. 
Does Mayor Perez have a financial interest which
prohlbits hirn frorn participating in decisions involving
the proposed Specific Pl.an?


RICHARDS WATSON & GERSHON
BLanca Breeze, Esq.
September 25, 1992
Page 4
2. 
Does the anslrer to the first question depend on whether
a redevelopment project, covering the same area as the
specific PIan, aLso is adopted?
We would appreciate your earliest response to this
matter. As mentioned above, the first meeting involving the
Specific Plan is scheduled for october L9, and the l,layor v/ould
like to participate in this inportant natter if there is no
prohibited conflict of interest.
Very truly yours,
/&6.k
I,lichael Estrada
cc: Mayor Perez [],5900 East Uain Street,
La Puente, California 
9A7 441
Robert G. Gutierrez, CiEy Manager
Carol W. Lynch, City Attorney
IilE : me
1090773

sections.question: 1. Does Mayor Perez have a financial interest which prohibits him from participating in decisions involving the proposed Specific Plan? 2. Does the answer to the first question depend on whether a redevelopment project, covering the same area as the specific Plan, also is adopted?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Mayor Perez has a disqualifying financial interest in decisions regarding a proposed Downtown Specific Plan, given that his wife is employed by a business located in the Plan Area that could potentially be affected by future implementation through property demolition.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter (not an FPPC response). The document contains two numbered questions for FPPC advice. No conclusion/answer section present as this is a request document. The letter provides extensive factual background about the Specific Plan, the business location, and potential future impacts.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: 1. Does Mayor Perez have a financial interest which prohibits him from participating in decisions involving the proposed Specific Plan? 2. Does the answer to the first question depend on whether a redevelopment project, covering the same area as the specific Plan, also is adopted?

embedding.summary: City Attorney's request for formal written advice from the FPPC regarding whether Mayor Louis R. Perez has a prohibited financial interest in participating in decisions regarding a proposed Downtown Specific Plan for La Puente, where his wife is employed by a business in the plan area.
